seoTitle: How to Use Pay in 4 With PayPal (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to split eligible online purchases into four interest-free installments using PayPal's Pay in 4 checkout option.
intro: PayPal's Pay in 4 lets you split qualifying purchases between $30 and $1,500 into four interest-free payments instead of paying the full amount upfront. This guide walks through finding the option at checkout and completing your first installment. It's useful for shoppers who want more flexibility without applying for traditional credit.

steps.heading: Shop at an eligible retailer
steps.body: Add items to your cart on a store that accepts PayPal and proceed to checkout.

steps.heading: Select PayPal at checkout
steps.body: Choose PayPal as your payment method and log in through the secure portal.

steps.heading: Find the Pay Later section
steps.body: Scroll past your default balance option to locate the specialized Pay Later section.

steps.heading: Choose Pay in 4
steps.body: Click the Pay in 4 option to open the installment authorization form and review your bi-weekly payment schedule.

steps.heading: Agree to terms and pay your first installment
steps.body: Accept the loan disclosures, pass the quick eligibility check, and select your funding card to pay the initial 25% down payment.

faq.question: Does Pay in 4 charge interest?
faq.answer: No, Pay in 4 is interest-free as long as payments are made on the standard bi-weekly schedule.

faq.question: Is there a credit check to use Pay in 4?
faq.answer: PayPal runs a quick soft credit eligibility check that doesn't typically affect your credit score.

faq.question: What purchase amounts qualify?
faq.answer: Eligible purchases generally range between $30 and $1,500, though exact limits can vary by account.

faq.question: What happens if I miss a payment?
faq.answer: Missed payments may result in late fees, so it's worth setting up autopay through the app to stay on schedule.
